name: AIOps Noise Reduction Workflow (phased)
serviceNowSurface: Event Management cleanup phase plus Now Assist for AIOps activation phase
trigger: ITOM lead initiates the noise reduction program
businessOutcome: Bring the NOC alert-to-incident noise ratio to a level where dashboards are trustworthy, so the operator team stops bypassing the platform with client-side filters.
steps: Phase 1: read Event Rules and alert volume; identify redundant or superseded rules.
Phase 1: surface event sources without a health-check probe.
Phase 1: retire redundant Event Rules via update set with ITOM lead approval; add health checks.
Phase 2: collect a 90-day clean baseline; do not activate AIOps yet.
Phase 2: define exclusion windows for month-end, payroll, and other seasonality.
Phase 3: activate Now Assist AIOps anomaly detection in shadow mode; compare against operator-confirmed incidents.
Phase 3: promote to active after 4 weeks of NOC operator sign-off.
approvalPoints: ITOM lead approval to retire each Event Rule batch
Platform owner approval to enter phase 3
NOC operator sign-off before active mode
failureStates: Baseline window not yet 90 days: phase 3 blocks with a clear message and remaining-days counter.
Anomaly detection produces high false-positive rate (>20 percent on shadow data): block active promotion and feed the seasonality list.
Event source health check fails: source quarantined, alerts not auto-promoted to incidents until source recovers.
auditNotes: Event Rule retirement update set retained.
Baseline evidence (alert volume per source per day) archived for one year.
AIOps activation event recorded in AI Control Tower.
tests: Phase 3 blocked with under 90 days baseline.
Synthetic seasonality spike on payroll day does not surface as anomaly when in exclusion window.
Forged event from non-whitelisted source rejected.
Alert without Service Map binding does not auto-create an incident.
Final noise ratio target met on synthetic 90-day replay.
